question: In the Mem0 paper, what name is given to the memory-architecture variant that augments the vector store with an entity-relation graph, and what specific advantage does the paper attribute to that variant?
The τ-bench paper introduces a reliability metric beyond simple pass-rate. What is it called, and what does it measure?

The OSWorld benchmark paper exposes a set of executable computer-use tasks. Approximately how many task instances does the benchmark contain at release, and on which operating systems are they executed?
In the SWE-agent paper, what does 'ACI' stand for, and what design problem does it argue ACI solves?
The Agent Interoperability Protocols survey compares four interoperability protocols by name. What are they?
The Agentic RAG survey distinguishes several families of agentic-RAG patterns. List them as named in the paper.
In the AppWorld benchmark, roughly how many apps and how many tasks does the controllable world expose to the agent?
The UI-TARS paper reports results on a standard computer-use benchmark. Which benchmark is it, and what is the reported overall success rate?
OpenHands proposes an event-driven architecture for general-purpose agents. What is the name of its core abstraction that unifies agent steps?
The OS-MAP paper proposes a taxonomy of computer-using-agent capabilities. Along which two axes is the taxonomy organized?
How does the memory design proposed in Mem0 differ from the design proposed in A-MEM? Compare on at least two dimensions (storage structure, write/update policy, retrieval mechanism).
Both UI-TARS and UI-TARS-2 are end-to-end native GUI agents. Summarize the main training-pipeline difference between the two papers.

The Multi-Turn Multi-Agent Orchestration paper and the Multi-Agent Collaboration via Evolving Orchestration paper both argue multi-agent setups can outperform single LLMs. Contrast their orchestration strategies.

The 'Can LLM Agents Really Debate?' paper reports a controlled study of debate-style multi-agent setups. Contrast its findings with the optimistic framing in the Multi-Agent Collaboration Mechanisms survey, citing both.
Compare the Deep Research Agents survey and the 'Deep Research: A Survey of Autonomous Research Agents' paper. Where do their taxonomies most disagree?
Tau-bench and AppWorld both evaluate tool-using agents. How do they differ in how the simulated user behaves during evaluation?
Both OpenHands and SWE-agent expose tools for code-editing agents. Compare the granularity and design of the file-editing tool each paper proposes.

The Open and Reproducible Deep Research paper and From Web Search Towards Agentic Deep Research both emphasize reproducibility. Identify the specific reproducibility failure each one calls out, citing both.

Compare the role of a 'reflector' or self-critique step as it appears across any two deep-research-agent papers in the 2024-2026 corpus, and discuss whether the two papers agree on when reflection helps.
OpenHands and UI-TARS-2 both handle long-horizon agent execution. How do their approaches to preserving state across long trajectories differ?

Summarize the main long-term memory architectures proposed for LLM agents between 2024 and 2026, citing at least four distinct papers. Group them by storage paradigm (e.g. vector, graph, hierarchical, note-based) and identify the central design trade-off each addresses.

Across the 2024-2026 multi-agent literature, what are the recurring arguments for and against using multi-agent systems instead of a single stronger LLM? Cite at least four papers, and identify at least one empirical claim that the literature appears to disagree on.
What is the state of computer-use / GUI-agent evaluation as of late 2025? Cite at least four papers covering benchmarks, agent systems, and capability taxonomies.

Synthesize the architectures of at least four open or openly-described deep-research agent systems from 2024-2026. Identify which components (planner, retriever, reflector, synthesizer) appear in each and where the systems most differ.

Summarize the agent interoperability protocol landscape (MCP, A2A, ACP, ANP) as captured by 2024-2026 literature. Cite at least four sources and identify the trade-off most commonly discussed across them.
What does the 2024-2026 literature say about the role of reflection or self-critique loops in LLM agents? Cite at least four papers and surface any disagreement on when reflection helps versus hurts.
Across the SWE-agent / OpenHands / SWE-EVO line of work and related code-agent papers, what are the main proposed improvements to Agent-Computer Interfaces? Cite at least four sources.
What does the 2024-2026 agentic-RAG literature say about when hybrid retrieval (lexical + dense + reranking) beats dense-only retrieval, and when it does not? Cite at least four papers.
Identify at least four distinct open problems in agentic AI evaluation as articulated by 2024-2026 papers. Cite the papers that articulate each problem.
Across the 2024-2026 literature on agent memory, tool use, and reflection, surface at least four common failure modes that papers report observing in deployed LLM agents. Cite at least four papers.
